name: Project Sentinel
description_short: Non-profit housing services organization offering fair housing advocacy, tenant-landlord mediation, and HUD-certified homeowner counseling across Northern California since 1976.
description_long:
Project Sentinel is a non-profit organization founded in 1976 that operates as one of the largest housing services agencies in Northern California. Based on the website, the organization is dedicated to safeguarding housing availability and stability while promoting fairness and equality of housing for all people. They explicitly commit to serving diverse and underserved communities, including undocumented and unhoused individuals, with a stated mission of advancing housing education, advocacy, and dialogue.

The organization offers three primary service categories: Fair Housing Advocacy & Counseling focused on fighting housing discrimination through federal and state Fair Housing law enforcement; Tenant-Landlord Dispute Resolution designed to help de-escalate conflicts that could lead to displacement, eviction, and homelessness; and HUD-Certified Homeowner Counseling providing reverse mortgage counseling, foreclosure prevention, and first-time homebuyer education. Project Sentinel administers over 50 programs to help people with housing issues, with services available via phone at (800) 339-6043 during business hours (Mon-Fri, 9am-4:30pm PT), TTY support for deaf/hard of hearing individuals, and language services for limited English speakers.

Project Sentinel distinguishes itself through its comprehensive, non-profit model that explicitly eliminates barriers to service and takes all calls for assistance. The organization operates multiple service locations throughout Northern California, hosts regular in-person trainings and community tabling events focused on fair housing rights education, and offers specialized programming at libraries, community centers, and senior centers. Their commitment to underserved populations and stated dedication to "taking every call" indicates accessibility-focused service delivery rather than selective client engagement.

As a free non-profit service, Project Sentinel provides legitimate value for low-income and vulnerable housing consumers. The main limitation is geographic scope—services are explicitly limited to Northern California. Additionally, the website does not provide detailed outcome metrics, response times, or specific success rates for dispute resolution, making it difficult to assess effectiveness compared to alternative housing counseling providers. Consumers outside the Northern California service area would need to identify alternative HUD-approved housing counselors.
pros: HUD-certified homeowner counseling with specific focus on foreclosure prevention and reverse mortgage counseling
Free tenant-landlord dispute resolution and mediation services to prevent displacement and eviction
Fair housing advocacy and enforcement of federal and state fair housing laws at no cost
Explicitly serves undocumented, unhoused, and underserved communities without eligibility restrictions
Language services available for limited English speakers through Language Services Associates
TTY (7-1-1) accessibility for deaf and hard of hearing individuals
Over 50 programs administered across multiple service locations in Northern California
Community-based education through regular workshops, tabling events, and presentations at libraries and senior centers
cons: Services limited to Northern California only—not available nationwide
Website does not provide specific metrics, success rates, or average resolution timelines for disputes
Limited phone availability (Mon-Fri, 9am-4:30pm PT only) may not accommodate all scheduling needs
No details provided on wait times for counseling appointments or dispute resolution services
Website lacks information about specific outcomes or client satisfaction data for foreclosure prevention services
best_for: Northern California tenants facing eviction or landlord disputes seeking free mediation
First-time homebuyers in Northern California requiring HUD-certified education
Homeowners facing foreclosure seeking prevention counseling and financial hardship assistance
Renters and homeowners experiencing housing discrimination needing fair housing advocacy
diagnosis: Project Sentinel is best for Northern California residents (tenants, homeowners, and first-time buyers) seeking free, non-profit housing counseling, fair housing enforcement, or dispute resolution services. The primary limitation is strict geographic scope—this organization does not serve consumers outside Northern California, and interested out-of-area consumers should contact their local HUD-approved housing counselor or NFCC-certified credit counselor.
services: Fair housing advocacy and discrimination enforcement
Fair housing rights education and workshops
Conflict de-escalation counseling for housing disputes
First-time homebuyer education classes (online and in-person)
Mediation and conflict resolution training courses
Community tabling and outreach on housing rights
Landlord-tenant counseling at libraries and community centers
Language interpretation services for limited English speakers
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1490 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050
